content.pattern_name: Rigged Taxi Meter and Unannounced Detour
observed_behaviors: The taximeter jumps rapidly or runs extremely fast.
The driver takes an unnecessarily long route to inflate the meter price.
The driver refuses to use standard navigation maps.
preconditions: Hailing an unbranded or unofficial taxi outside tourist locations.
Driver refusing to agree on a fixed price before starting.
content.common_scenario: Tourist hails a random taxi near Hoan Kiem Lake. The driver claims the meter is standard, but the meter rate increases rapidly by 10x within 2 kilometers. When questioned, the driver locks doors or demands payment.
content.geographic_scope: Hanoi Old Quarter and major tourist spots
evidence: Traveler report of paying 500,000 VND for a 1.5 km trip near Hoan Kiem.
Independent blog post detailing rigged meters in Hanoi.
safe_actions: Use only major ride-hailing applications (e.g., Grab, Be, Xanh SM) to book transport.
Take reputable branded taxis (e.g., Mai Linh, Taxi Group) and verify the driver ID and company brand mark on the door.
Take a picture of the driver ID card and license plate before entering.

claims.value: Rigged meters and rapid rate increments
claims.quoted_evidence: meter jumps extremely fast to charge ten times normal fare

review_reasons: High-risk scam pattern warning requires verification before public release

retention_policy.reason: Quarterly review of active scam patterns
